Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are going to construct a Petri net for the insurance claim process described

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Steps 1 Create a Petri net object 2 Create places for the start and end and for each activity or between activities as needed 3 Create transitions for each activity CR DV FC CA AP P N CL RDS II 4 Connect places and transitions with arcs to represent the flow including the loops for RDS and II

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We note the following from the scenario The process starts with a claim C and then goes to Claim Registration CR After CR we have Document Verification DV After DV if documents are incomplete we go to Re Document Submission RDS and then back to DV After DV if complete we go to Fraud Check FC After FC if there are doubts we go to In depth Investigation II and then back to FC This can happen multiple times After FC if no doubts or after II we go to Claim Assessment CA

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: After CA we have Approval AP if the amount surpasses a threshold Otherwise we might skip AP The scenario says if the amount surpasses a certain threshold We are not given a condition for skipping so we assume that AP is always required But the scenario says if the amount surpasses a certain threshold Let us assume that the decision is made in the CA activity and then we have two transitions one for going to AP and one for going directly to Payment P

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: However the scenario does not specify that Since the problem is about building a Petri net we must make a reasonable assumption Let us clarify the steps as per the scenario 1 Claim Registration CR 2 Document Verification DV 3 Fraud Check FC 4 Claim Assessment CA 5 Approval AP conditional on amount threshold 6 Payment P 7 Notification N 8 Closure CL

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Since the condition for AP is not specified in terms of how to model it we will assume that after CA we have two possibilities If the amount is below threshold then we skip AP and go directly to P If the amount is above threshold then we go to AP and then to P

Name: Validation - IND
Text: However note that the scenario says must be approved by a senior manager if the amount surpasses a certain threshold So AP is only done conditionally

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: We can model this by having a decision after CA However the Petri net does not have conditions on transitions We can use silent transitions or we can have two transitions from the same place one for the condition and one for the other Since we are only building the structure we can have two transitions and rely on the fact that in practice the transition that is taken will be determined by the data

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Since the problem does not specify I will avoid silent transitions and use two transitions labeled P

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We will have the following places and transitions Places p_start p_after_CR p_after_DV p_after_FC p_after_CA p_after_AP p_paid p_after_N p_final And also for the loops p_after_RDS after re document submission which goes back to DV p_after_II after in depth investigation which goes back to FC

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Transitions CR from p_start to p_after_CR DV from p_after_CR to p_after_DV RDS from p_after_DV to p_after_RDS DV again from p_after_RDS to p_after_DV FC from p_after_DV to p_after_FC II from p_after_FC to p_after_II FC again from p_after_II to p_after_FC CA from p_after_FC to p_after_CA AP from p_after_CA to p_after_AP P direct from p_after_CA to p_paid P from p_after_AP to p_paid N from p_paid to p_after_N CL from p_after_N to p_final

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Initial marking one token in p_start Final marking one token in p_final

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Now we have to be cautious the above model has two transitions from p_after_DV t_RDS and t_FC1 This means that when a token is in p_after_DV both transitions are enabled In reality we want to choose one based on whether the documents are complete or not But in the Petri net structure we do not model the condition So we leave it as a non deterministic choice Similarly from p_after_FC we have two transitions t_II and t_CA This is acceptable for a Petri net structure

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Steps in code 1 Create the Petri net 2 Create places and add them to the net 3 Create transitions with labels and add them to the net 4 Add arcs from places to transitions and from transitions to places We will use the petri_utils to add arcs Let us code accordingly
